title: Additive (Bahdanau) Attention
concept: Bahdanau attention calculates similarity scores using a small feed-forward network, learning how to score query-key matches.

exercises.prompt: What does Bahdanau (Additive) Attention use to calculate the similarity between queries and keys?
exercises.explain: Bahdanau attention uses a small feed-forward network to calculate similarity scores, making it a flexible, learnable approach.

cards.title: Flexible Focus
cards.body: How does a computer decide which words in a sentence are connected? Instead of using fixed formulas, what if we let the computer learn its own custom scoring system? That is the secret behind Bahdanau Attention!

cards.title: What is Additive Attention?
cards.body: Additive (or Bahdanau) attention calculates similarity scores between a query and a key using a small, trainable feed-forward neural network. Instead of using a fixed mathematical trick (like dot-product), it learns how to score the relationship over time.

cards.title: The Smart Referee
cards.body: Think of a traditional matching game where you use rigid rules to pair items. Bahdanau attention is like hiring a smart referee who looks at both the clue (query) and the options (keys) and uses their judgment to score how well they match. Over time, the referee learns exactly what features make a good pair.

cards.title: Translating French to English
cards.body: When translating 'Le chat noir' to 'The black cat', the AI needs to focus on 'noir' when predicting 'black'. With Bahdanau attention, the model trains a mini network to realize that the query 'black' and the key 'noir' are highly compatible, dynamically adjusting the connection strength.

cards.title: Not All Math is Rigid!
cards.body: People often think that all attention mechanisms calculate similarity the same way (like multiplying vectors). In reality, Bahdanau attention proves that using a learnable neural network layer to calculate attention can be much more flexible and powerful for complex languages.

cards.title: Fixed vs. Learnable Similarity
cards.body: Comparing fixed-formula attention with learnable additive attention.

left.text: Uses a fixed mathematical formula (multiplication) to score similarity. No extra parameters to learn.

right.text: Uses a small neural network layer. It learns and updates how to score connections during training.

exercises.prompt: How does Bahdanau (Additive) Attention differ from simple Dot-Product Attention?

options.text: It uses a trainable neural network to score matches.
It simply multiplies the query and key together directly.
It ignores the keys and only looks at the query.
It randomly guesses which words are important.

exercises.explain: Unlike dot-product attention which multiplies queries and keys directly, Bahdanau attention uses a small feed-forward network with trainable weights to calculate scores.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Bahdanau attention has parameters that are learned and updated during model training.
exercises.explain: Yes! Because it uses a neural network layer, it has weights that are optimized during training.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe how Bahdanau attention calculates scores.
exercises.sentence: Bahdanau attention uses a small feed-forward neural ______ to learn the best way to match queries and keys.
exercises.explain: Bahdanau attention processes the query and key through a small feed-forward neural network to calculate the final attention weight.

exercises.prompt: Identify the system that is using Additive (Bahdanau) Attention:

options.text: An AI model that uses a learnable neural network layer to dynamically calculate how much focus to put on each input word.
An AI model that multiplies query and key vectors directly without any learnable parameters.
An AI model that reads text sequentially and cannot jump back to look at previous words.

exercises.explain: Option B is incorrect because it describes simple dot-product attention, not the learnable feed-forward network approach of Bahdanau. Option C describes no attention at all. Option A correctly describes Bahdanau attention.

exercises.prompt: Why is Bahdanau attention called "additive" or "learnable" attention?

options.text: It uses fixed, hardcoded math rules like multiplication to calculate scores.
It uses a small feed-forward network to learn the scoring function.
It only looks at values and completely ignores the query.
It removes the need for weights entirely.

exercises.explain: Bahdanau attention doesn't assume a simple math formula like multiplication is enough. Instead, it feeds the query and key into a tiny neural network layer, learning the optimal way to compute attention weights during training.
